heroCompanyDescription: Ora Banda Mining Limited (ASX: OBM) is an Australian gold exploration and development company focused on its project portfolio in the Ora Banda district of Western Australia, a historically productive gold region within the broader Eastern Goldfields. The company has recently undergone a transformational exploration phase, highlighted by a tenfold increase in the Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces (announced 2026-03-10), alongside the expansion of the Little Gem deposit and the discovery of the new Sapphire Trend (announced 2026-03-11).

skew.rationale: The probability distribution is skewed modestly to the upside, driven by the combination of a transformational resource upgrade, an active exploration pipeline, and a record gold price environment that materially improves project economics. The primary constraint on a more aggressive bullish skew is the absence of financial disclosure, which prevents assessment of balance sheet capacity to fund development and introduces meaningful dilution risk.

verdict.text: At A$1.50, OBM is priced as a credible but unfunded gold developer in the early stages of a resource re-rating cycle. The market is right to assign a significant discount to the 1.33 million ounce Round Dam resource given the absence of a funded development plan, but the combination of record gold prices at US$5,027.9/oz, an active exploration pipeline, and a pragmatic third-party processing arrangement suggests the risk-reward is modestly skewed to the upside from current levels. The primary analytical constraint is the complete absence of financial disclosure -- without balance sheet data, production costs, or cash flow information, it is impossible to determine whether OBM can self-fund its development ambitions or will require repeated dilutive equity raises that erode per-share value. We initiate coverage with a modestly bullish skew, acknowledging that the next 12 months of newsflow -- particularly any pre-feasibility study, quarterly cash flow reports, and further exploration results -- will be decisive in determining whether the N1 bull case or N3 bear case prevails.

featuredRationale: The hypothesis distribution assigns 40% to the base case of continued exploration progress with an extended development timeline, 30% to a full resource re-rating bull case, 22% to a bear case driven by capital constraints or gold price reversal, and 8% to a tail catalyst scenario involving M&A or institutional discovery. The net skew is modestly bullish, with upside scenarios (N1 + N4) totalling 38% against a 22% bear case, but the base case dominates given the significant information gaps around OBM's financial position.

identity.overview: Ora Banda Mining Limited (ASX: OBM) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: The tenfold increase in Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces, combined with the Little Gem expansion and new Sapphire Trend discovery, catalyses a sustained re-rating of OBM as a credible mid-tier gold developer. With gold at US$5,028/oz and rising, the economics of developing these resources become compelling, attracting institutional interest and broker coverage upgrades. This scenario requires continued exploration success, a clear development pathway, and sustained gold prices above US$4,500/oz.
supporting: Round Dam Mineral Resource grew tenfold to 1.33 million ounces (ASX, 2026-03-10), a transformational upgrade for a company of OBM's size
Gold price at US$5,027.9/oz (+3.0% on the day of analysis), providing exceptional margin leverage for any ounces brought into production
New Sapphire Trend discovery and Little Gem expansion (ASX, 2026-03-11) suggest the exploration pipeline remains active and open-ended
Formalisation of third-party processing OSA (ASX, 2026-03-13) reduces near-term capex burden and accelerates potential production timelines
OBM's 52-week range of A$0.64 to A$1.67 shows the market has already begun re-rating the stock from deeply depressed levels
contradicting: No financial statements or production data are available to confirm the company can fund development of the expanded resource base
Recent securities issuance (ASX, 2026-02-27) raises dilution risk if further capital raises are required at current or lower prices
No broker consensus or independent valuation is available to anchor a fair value estimate for the expanded resource
Gold price at US$5,028/oz is historically elevated and mean-reversion risk is material

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: OBM continues to deliver exploration newsflow and resource growth, but the path from resource to production remains multi-year and capital-intensive. The third-party processing OSA provides a near-term production outlet for some material, but the bulk of the 1.33 million ounce Round Dam resource requires significant further study, permitting, and funding. The stock consolidates in the A$1.30 to A$1.70 range as the market waits for a definitive development decision or pre-feasibility study.
supporting: Conversion of MOU to OSA for third-party processing (ASX, 2026-03-13) provides a pragmatic near-term production pathway without requiring full mine development capex
Sustained gold price above US$5,000/oz supports project economics and reduces the hurdle rate for development decisions
Active exploration programme with multiple targets (Little Gem, Sapphire Trend, Round Dam) suggests ongoing newsflow to support investor interest
Current price of A$1.50 sits near the middle of the 52-week range, consistent with a market in a wait-and-see mode
contradicting: Absence of financial data (revenue, EBITDA, debt) makes it impossible to assess balance sheet capacity to fund development
No broker research or independent feasibility work is publicly available to validate the development case
Securities issuance activity suggests the company may be reliant on equity markets for ongoing funding, creating dilution overhang

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: OBM's expanded resource base proves difficult to monetise if gold prices correct materially from current elevated levels or if the company is unable to raise sufficient capital on acceptable terms. The third-party processing OSA may generate limited near-term cash flow, but without a funded development plan for Round Dam, the resource remains stranded. Repeated equity dilution at low prices erodes per-share value and investor confidence, pushing the stock back toward the lower end of its 52-week range.
supporting: No financial statements are available, making it impossible to confirm OBM has sufficient cash or debt capacity to fund development
Recent 'Application for quotation of securities' (ASX, 2026-02-27) indicates ongoing reliance on equity issuance, which is dilutive
Gold at US$5,028/oz is historically extreme; a reversion toward US$3,000-3,500/oz would materially impair project economics
Absence of broker coverage and institutional following limits the pool of capital available to fund future raises
contradicting: The tenfold resource upgrade to 1.33 million ounces provides a substantial asset base that retains value even at lower gold prices
Third-party processing OSA reduces immediate capex requirements and provides a lower-risk production pathway
Current gold price environment is the most favourable in history, reducing the probability of a near-term severe correction

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A larger gold producer or private equity acquirer recognises the value of OBM's rapidly growing resource base in a record gold price environment and launches a takeover bid or strategic investment at a significant premium to the current A$1.50 price. Alternatively, a major institutional fund initiates a cornerstone position, triggering a wave of broker initiations and retail interest. This tail scenario requires an external catalyst beyond OBM's own operational execution.
supporting: Record gold prices above US$5,000/oz make resource acquisition highly attractive for larger producers seeking to replenish depleting reserves
OBM's 1.33 million ounce Round Dam resource, combined with active exploration upside at Little Gem and Sapphire Trend, represents a compelling acquisition target
The stock's 52-week low of A$0.64 and current price of A$1.50 suggest the market has not yet fully priced the resource growth, leaving room for a bid premium
contradicting: No evidence of M&A interest, strategic investor discussions, or takeover speculation in available announcements or media
Absence of broker coverage and limited institutional following reduces the likelihood of a near-term cornerstone investment
Unclear capital structure and potential dilution overhang may deter acquirers until the balance sheet is better understood

narrative.theNarrative: [Coverage Initiated 17-Mar-26 20:06 AEST] The dominant narrative driving Ora Banda Mining (ASX: OBM) at A$1.50 is one of rapid resource discovery in a record gold price environment, with the market progressively upgrading its view of the company's asset base following a series of material exploration announcements. The centrepiece of this narrative is the tenfold increase in the Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces, announced on 2026-03-10, which transformed OBM from a small-scale explorer into a company with a resource base of genuine development scale. This was followed within 24 hours by the announcement of Little Gem expansion drilling and the new Sapphire Trend discovery (2026-03-11), reinforcing the view that OBM's tenement package has significant further exploration upside. The formalisation of a third-party processing OSA on 2026-03-13 adds a pragmatic near-term production dimension, suggesting management is focused on monetising the asset base rather than simply accumulating resources. All of this is occurring against the backdrop of gold at US$5,027.9/oz, a historically unprecedented price level that dramatically improves the economics of even modest production scenarios. The key risk to this narrative is the absence of any financial disclosure in the available data -- OBM's balance sheet, cash position, debt levels, and production costs are unknown, making it impossible to assess whether the company can self-fund development or will require repeated dilutive equity raises. The stock's position at A$1.50, near the top of its A$0.64 to A$1.67 52-week range, suggests the market is cautiously optimistic but has not yet made a full commitment to the development thesis. The next major test of the narrative will be a pre-feasibility study or development decision for Round Dam, which would either validate the bull case or expose the funding gap that the bear case anticipates.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$1.50
priceImplication.content:
What A$1.50 appears to embed:

• Partial credit for the 1.33 million ounce Round Dam resource -- the market is likely applying a steep discount (possibly 80-90% to in-situ value) reflecting development risk, funding uncertainty, and timeline

• No near-term production premium -- the third-party processing OSA is acknowledged but not yet valued as a material cash flow contributor, given the absence of disclosed processing volumes or economics

• Exploration optionality at a modest premium -- the Little Gem expansion and Sapphire Trend discovery are priced as early-stage upside rather than defined resources

• Gold price assumption likely conservative relative to spot -- at US$5,028/oz spot, the market is probably discounting to a long-run gold price assumption of US$3,000-3,500/oz for valuation purposes, consistent with industry practice

• Dilution risk is priced in -- the recent securities issuance and absence of disclosed cash reserves suggest the market is assuming further equity raises will be required, capping the per-share re-rating

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base for this initiation is materially constrained by the absence of financial disclosure. The strongest evidence domains are corporate communications and regulatory filings, where OBM has been active with market-sensitive ASX announcements covering resource upgrades, exploration results, and the OSA formalisation -- all of which are verified, dated, and specific. The economic data domain is well-supported by the observable gold price of US$5,027.9/oz. However, the critical domains of broker research, financial statements, competitor analysis, leadership and governance, and ownership structure are either absent or provide only indirect signals. The 'Application for quotation of securities' filing (2026-02-27) is the only capital structure data point available, and without the accompanying details, it is impossible to assess dilution quantum or balance sheet health. The absence of earnings data, production costs, and cash flow information means this analysis is necessarily resource-focused rather than financially grounded, and the hypothesis probability weightings carry wider-than-normal uncertainty bands.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current bullish exploration narrative is moderately stable in the near term but faces several potential destabilisers. The most immediate threat is a material correction in the gold price from its historically elevated US$5,028/oz level -- a reversion to US$3,000/oz or below would significantly impair project economics and likely trigger a re-rating of the stock back toward the lower end of its 52-week range. A second destabiliser would be a capital raise at a price below A$1.50, which would signal balance sheet stress and trigger dilution-driven selling. Conversely, the narrative could be significantly strengthened by the release of a pre-feasibility study for Round Dam, the disclosure of strong third-party processing volumes and economics under the OSA, or the announcement of a strategic investor or offtake partner. The next major test of the narrative is likely to be OBM's next quarterly activities report, which should provide production data, cash flow information, and an update on the development timeline for Round Dam.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality — from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary — ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: Ora Banda Mining Limited (OBM) released a 'Company & Site Visit Presentation' on 2026-03-13, likely highlighting recent operational successes and future growth prospects. This communication follows significant resource upgrades and drilling discoveries, aiming to inform and attract investors.
cards.tension: As a company-issued presentation, the content is inherently promotional and may selectively emphasize positive aspects while potentially downplaying risks or challenges. Specific details of the presentation's content are not available to verify claims.

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: OBM announced a tenfold increase in its Round Dam Mineral Resource to 1.33 million ounces on 2026-03-10, a significant upgrade for a gold miner. Additionally, an 'Application for quotation of securities' was filed on 2026-02-27, indicating recent capital activity or share issuance.
cards.tension: While the resource upgrade is positive, the financial implications, such as development costs, capital expenditure requirements, or the economic viability of extracting these ounces, are not detailed. The impact of the new securities on shareholder dilution or the company's balance sheet is also unclear without further information.
cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Round Dam Mineral Resource Grows Tenfold to 1.33M Ounces' (2026-03-10); ASX Announcement: 'Application for quotation of securities - OBM' (2026-02-27)

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: No specific broker research or consensus estimates for OBM are provided in the available data. However, given the recent market-sensitive announcements regarding substantial resource growth and new discoveries, it is highly probable that brokers are in the process of updating their models and price targets.
cards.tension: Without actual broker reports or analyst coverage, any assumptions about market expectations or valuation models remain speculative. The absence of this data limits the ability to gauge external professional sentiment and financial projections.
cards.source: No direct source provided for broker research; inferred from recent market-sensitive ASX announcements.

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: The provided data does not include specific competitor disclosures or information regarding OBM's market positioning relative to its peers. However, the significant increase in OBM's mineral resource to 1.33 million ounces suggests a strengthening competitive profile within the gold sector.
cards.tension: Without direct comparative data on peer operational costs, resource grades, or development timelines, it is challenging to fully assess OBM's competitive advantages or disadvantages. The impact of its 3rd party processing agreement on its competitive standing is also not quantifiable.
cards.source: No direct source provided for competitor disclosures; inferred from OBM's resource growth.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: The price of gold (GC=F) is currently US$5027.9, having increased by +3.0% today. This strong and rising commodity price environment provides a highly favourable backdrop for gold producers like OBM, potentially enhancing revenue and profitability margins.
cards.tension: While the current gold price is robust, commodity markets are inherently volatile and subject to rapid fluctuations driven by macroeconomic factors, geopolitical events, and currency movements. A sustained downturn in gold prices could significantly impact OBM's project economics.

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: OBM announced the 'Conversion of MOU to OSA for 3rd Party Processing' on 2026-03-13, formalizing an agreement for external ore processing. This indicates a strategic move to optimize operational efficiency, potentially reduce capital expenditure, or monetize resources that might otherwise be uneconomical for internal processing.
cards.tension: The specific terms and conditions of the Offtake and Processing Agreement (OSA), such as processing rates, costs, duration, and revenue split, are not disclosed. Without these details, the financial benefits or potential limitations of this third-party arrangement cannot be fully assessed.
cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Conversion of MOU to OSA for 3rd Party Processing' (2026-03-13)

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: No specific academic research or peer-reviewed studies directly related to OBM's operations, geological setting, or specific projects are provided in the available data. General academic research on gold exploration often highlights the increasing challenges and costs associated with discovering new, high-grade deposits globally.
cards.tension: The absence of specific academic research makes it difficult to contextualize OBM's recent discoveries (Little Gem, Sapphire Trend) and significant resource upgrades (Round Dam) within broader industry trends, geological models, or technological advancements in exploration and mining.
cards.source: No direct source provided for academic research; general industry knowledge.

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: No recent news headlines, financial media coverage, or social media commentary specifically mentioning OBM are provided in the input data. This suggests a current lack of broad public or media attention, despite the recent market-sensitive announcements regarding resource growth and new discoveries.
cards.tension: The absence of media coverage could indicate that OBM is not yet widely followed by financial news outlets, or that the market has not fully processed the implications of its recent operational updates. This lack of external commentary makes it challenging to gauge broader market sentiment or public perception.
cards.source: No news headlines or social media provided.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: The provided data does not contain any specific information regarding OBM's leadership changes, insider trading activities, or detailed governance practices. While a 'Company & Site Visit Presentation' was held on 2026-03-13, details about the management team's specific insights or strategic direction are not available.
cards.tension: Without insights into leadership stability, executive compensation, insider share movements, or the robustness of governance structures, it is challenging to assess the quality of management or potential risks associated with corporate oversight and decision-making.
cards.source: No direct source provided for leadership and governance.

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: OBM filed an 'Application for quotation of securities' on 2026-02-27, indicating a recent issuance of new shares. This suggests either a capital raise to fund ongoing operations and development, or the conversion of existing financial instruments, which will impact the company's capital structure.
cards.tension: The specific details of the securities issued, including the number of shares, the issue price, and the precise purpose of the issuance, are not provided. Without this information, it is impossible to determine the extent of shareholder dilution or the exact impact on the company's cash position and ownership structure.
cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Application for quotation of securities - OBM' (2026-02-27)

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity — they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Quarterly cash flow report disclosing OBM's cash balance and operating cash flow from third-party processing under the OSA -- this is the single most important near-term data point to discriminate between the funded development bull case (N1) and the capital constraint bear case (N3)
rows.currentReading: Unknown -- no financial statements available as of 2026-03-17; next quarterly report expected April 2026

rows.evidence: Gold price trajectory over the next 6-12 months -- sustained gold above US$4,500/oz materially supports project economics and the bull case, while a correction below US$3,500/oz would impair Round Dam's development economics and shift probability toward the bear case
rows.currentReading: US$5,027.9/oz (+3.0% on 2026-03-17) -- currently strongly supportive of N1

rows.evidence: Announcement of a Scoping Study, Pre-Feasibility Study, or strategic investor/offtake partner for Round Dam -- this would be the definitive signal that OBM is transitioning from explorer to developer and would discriminate sharply between the base case (N2) and the full re-rating bull case (N1)
rows.currentReading: No study announced as of 2026-03-17; OSA formalisation is a positive precursor but not a development decision

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Exploration activity level -- OBM is clearly active across multiple targets (Round Dam, Little Gem, Sapphire Trend), but the pace of drilling does not by itself discriminate between the bull and bear cases; what matters is the economic quality of what is found and whether it can be funded

• Number of ASX announcements -- the volume of recent market-sensitive announcements is high, but announcement frequency is a poor predictor of financial outcomes and does not discriminate between hypotheses

• 52-week price range -- the A$0.64 to A$1.67 range confirms high volatility and market interest but does not discriminate between the competing scenarios for the next 12 months

• Third-party processing OSA existence -- the formalisation of the OSA is a positive signal, but without disclosed volumes and economics, it does not discriminate between a scenario where it generates material cash flow (N1/N2) and one where it is marginal (N3)

• Gold price level in isolation -- while gold at US$5,028/oz is supportive of all development scenarios, the price level alone does not discriminate between OBM-specific outcomes; what matters is OBM's cost structure relative to the gold price, which is currently unknown

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If the quarterly report discloses positive cash flow from third-party processing operations and a cash balance above A$20 million
conditions.then: Then the base case is validated, dilution risk recedes, and the stock is likely to re-rate toward A$1.80-2.00 as the market gains confidence in OBM's ability to self-fund near-term operations

conditions.if: If the quarterly report reveals a cash balance below A$10 million with minimal or no production revenue from the OSA
conditions.then: Then the bear case gains traction, a dilutive capital raise becomes probable, and the stock risks re-testing A$1.00-1.20 as investors price in equity dilution

cards.name: Round Dam Scoping Study or PFS Release

conditions.if: If OBM releases a Scoping Study or Pre-Feasibility Study for Round Dam showing a post-tax NPV above A$500 million at a conservative gold price assumption
conditions.then: Then the N1 bull case is strongly supported, institutional interest is likely to accelerate, and the stock could re-rate toward A$2.50-3.00 as the market begins to price a funded development pathway

conditions.if: If no Scoping Study or PFS is announced by end of 2026, or if a study reveals prohibitive capital costs or low-grade economics
conditions.then: Then the market will question whether the 1.33 million ounce resource can be economically developed, the narrative stalls, and the stock likely consolidates below A$1.30

cards.source: Expected ASX announcement; no specific date disclosed by OBM as of 2026-03-17

cards.name: Third-Party Processing OSA Volume and Economics Disclosure

conditions.if: If OBM discloses strong processing volumes and favourable economics under the OSA, with annualised revenue run-rate above A$30 million
conditions.then: Then the OSA is confirmed as a material cash flow contributor, reducing reliance on equity markets and supporting a re-rating toward the upper end of the base case range

conditions.if: If OSA volumes are minimal or the economics are disclosed as marginal, with processing costs consuming the majority of revenue
conditions.then: Then the near-term production thesis is undermined, the company remains dependent on equity funding, and the bear case probability increases materially

cards.source: ASX Announcement: 'Conversion of MOU to OSA for 3rd Party Processing' (2026-03-13); expected further disclosure in quarterly reports

Low -- no broker reports or consensus estimates available

High -- gold price is observable and current
Low -- OSA terms and volumes are undisclosed
Low -- no project-specific geological or technical studies available
Low -- no media coverage or social sentiment data available
Low -- no management team data, insider activity, or governance disclosures available
Low -- securities issuance noted but quantum, price, and purpose are undisclosed

couldntAssess: Balance sheet health, cash position, and debt levels
All-in sustaining cost (AISC) per ounce for any current or planned production
Management team quality, track record, and insider share ownership
Institutional shareholder register and ownership concentration

Permitting status and timeline for Round Dam development
Capital expenditure requirements for Round Dam development
Broker consensus price targets and earnings estimates
Market capitalisation (not provided in input data)
gaps.analyticalLimitations: This initiation is materially constrained by the absence of financial statements, production data, and balance sheet information for OBM. The hypothesis probability weightings are therefore based primarily on resource and exploration data rather than financial fundamentals, which is an inherently weaker analytical foundation for assessing investment merit. The gold price is the dominant external variable in this analysis, and its current historically elevated level introduces significant mean-reversion risk that is difficult to quantify without a long-run price assumption anchored by OBM's own cost structure. The absence of broker research means there is no independent cross-check on the resource valuation or development economics. Investors should treat all price targets and scenario valuations in this report as highly indicative pending the release of OBM's quarterly cash flow report, financial statements, and any Scoping Study or Pre-Feasibility Study for Round Dam.

trend.structure: Price in uptrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$1.50 per share, the market appears to be pricing OBM as an early-stage developer with a credible but unfunded resource base, acknowledging the tenfold Round Dam upgrade and exploration momentum while discounting for the multi-year development timeline and capital requirements. The current price implies the market is not yet assigning full in-situ value to the 1.33 million ounce resource, likely applying a steep discount for technical, funding, and execution risk. The stock's recovery from A$0.64 to A$1.50 over the past 52 weeks suggests the market has progressively upgraded its view of OBM's exploration potential, but has not yet made a definitive call on development viability. At US$5,028/oz gold, even a modest production scenario would generate substantial cash flow, suggesting the current price may still undervalue the optionality embedded in OBM's asset base if a funded development plan emerges.
hero.skew_description: The hypothesis distribution assigns 40% to the base case of continued exploration progress with an extended development timeline, 30% to a full resource re-rating bull case, 22% to a bear case driven by capital constraints or gold price reversal, and 8% to a tail catalyst scenario involving M&A or institutional discovery. The net skew is modestly bullish, with upside scenarios (N1 + N4) totalling 38% against a 22% bear case, but the base case dominates given the significant information gaps around OBM's financial position.

position_in_range.note: World prices are indicative -- pending full W1-W4 valuation build. Bear case reflects capital constraint and gold price correction scenario. Bull case reflects funded development decision at current gold prices. Catalyst case reflects M&A bid or major institutional re-rating.

next_decision_point.event: Quarterly Activities and Cash Flow Report (March 2026 Quarter)
next_decision_point.metric: Cash position, production volumes under third-party processing OSA, and any update on Round Dam development timeline or pre-feasibility study commencement
next_decision_point.thresholds: Positive: Cash balance above A$20 million with positive operating cash flow from OSA processing would validate the base case and support a move toward A$1.80-2.00. Negative: Cash balance below A$10 million with no production revenue would confirm capital constraint fears and risk a re-test of A$1.00-1.20. A pre-feasibility study announcement would be a step-change catalyst for the bull case.
